evals.prompt: Bootstrap this ML.NET repository with a full AI-native layer
evals.expected_output: 14 files generated across 5 layers, all customized with ML.NET-specific patterns (Contracts.Check*, IChannel, BaseTestClass, xUnit, ./build.sh)
expectations: AGENTS.md was created or verified at the repo root
CLAUDE.md was created or verified at the repo root
.github/copilot-instructions.md was created or verified
At least 5 agent files exist in .github/agents/ with valid YAML frontmatter containing 'name' and 'description'
At least 2 prompt files exist in .github/prompts/ with 'mode: agent' in frontmatter
.github/workflows/copilot-setup-steps.yml exists and contains a build step
.github/hooks/copilot-pre-commit.sh exists and is executable
AGENTS.md contains a real test example copied from the repo (not a generic placeholder)
copilot-instructions.md contains the correct build command for this repo
The implementer agent references the repo's actual validation pattern (Contracts.Check* for ML.NET)
The test-writer agent references the repo's actual test base class (BaseTestClass for ML.NET)
The security-reviewer agent mentions native interop risks (specific to ML.NET)

evals.prompt: I have a brand new ASP.NET Core Web API project with xUnit tests and ILogger. Make it AI-native.
evals.expected_output: 14 files generated with ASP.NET Core-specific customizations: ILogger<T> logging, middleware patterns, XSS/CSRF security surfaces
expectations: The discovery phase ran commands to detect the stack before generating files
copilot-instructions.md mentions 'dotnet build' as the build command (no build.sh for typical ASP.NET projects)
The implementer agent references ILogger<T> for logging (not IChannel)
The security-reviewer agent mentions XSS, CSRF, SQL injection (web-specific threats)
The test-writer agent references xUnit patterns with the correct base class discovered from the repo
No existing files were overwritten (check-before-create behavior)
A structured completion report was output listing all files created

evals.prompt: This repo already has AGENTS.md and copilot-instructions.md but no agents directory. Add the missing pieces.
evals.expected_output: Only missing files created; existing files enhanced rather than replaced
expectations: The skill detected existing AGENTS.md and copilot-instructions.md
AGENTS.md was NOT overwritten or replaced
copilot-instructions.md was NOT overwritten or replaced
Agent files were created in .github/agents/
Prompt files were created in .github/prompts/
The completion report mentions that existing files were preserved

evals.prompt: Bootstrap a .NET class library that uses NUnit and Guard clauses. It has no build scripts, just dotnet build.
evals.expected_output: Files customized for NUnit test patterns, Guard.Against validation, and simple dotnet build
expectations: The test-writer agent uses [Test] and [TestCase] attributes (NUnit, not xUnit's [Fact]/[Theory])
The test-writer agent uses Assert.That assertion style (NUnit, not Assert.Equal)
The implementer agent references Guard.Against.Null pattern (not Contracts.Check*)
copilot-instructions.md shows 'dotnet build' as the build command
The security-reviewer agent mentions deserialization and path traversal (library threats, not web threats)
